vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1111: Upgrade beads to v1.0.4 and remove redundant wrapper scaffolding

plan.title: Upgrade beads to v1.0.4 and remove redundant wrapper scaffolding
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

verification.notes:
Acceptance criteria check FAILED — 29/29 AC incomplete:

### Bump bd install version pin from 1.0.2 to 1.0.4 (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] src/cli/commands/install.ts recommendedVersion encodes 1.0.4 (10004)
  - [ ] No surviving 1.0.2 literals in install.ts (URLs, comments, error strings)
  - [ ] `bd --version` on the developer machine reports 1.0.4 (or later patch on 1.0.x) after running pan install
  - [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

### Replace `bd list --json --limit 0` connectivity probe with `bd ping --json` (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] The connectivity probe in createBeadsFromVBrief uses `bd ping --json` (not `bd list`)
  - [ ] Probe timeout remains 8s; cwd remains workspacePath
  - [ ] Mocks in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts updated; `vitest run src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts` passes

### Simplify createBeadsFromVBrief recovery: on probe failure, run `bd doctor --fix` once then retry (6/6 incomplete)
  - [ ] On `bd ping` failure, the code invokes `bd doctor --fix` exactly once before retrying the probe
  - [ ] The stale-artifact heuristic (staleArtifacts array, presentStale check, dolt-residue diagnostic) is deleted from src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts
  - [ ] The redirect-vs-main-beads branch logic in the catch block is collapsed into a single retry/error-return path
  - [ ] The fresh-install bd-init fallback (no redirect, no main .beads/) is preserved with identical argv and follow-on `git config` / `bd config set` calls
  - [ ] A code comment near createBeadsFromVBrief explains why the heuristic was removed (refs beads v1.0.3 dolt auto-recovery)
  - [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

### Update create-beads.test.ts to match the simplified recovery surface (5/5 incomplete)
  - [ ] All mocks reference `bd ping` (with `--json`), not the old `bd list --json --limit 0` probe
  - [ ] Tests for the stale-artifact heuristic are removed (no remaining references to `metadata.json` / `embeddeddolt` / `presentStale`)
  - [ ] There is a test asserting `bd doctor --fix` is called once between a failed probe and a successful retry, and the function returns `success: true`
  - [ ] There is a test asserting `success: false` with non-empty `errors` when both probe attempts fail
  - [ ] `vitest run src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts` passes

### Add corruption-recovery integration test (issue AC #5) (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] Test simulates a `table not found: issues` probe failure followed by `bd doctor --fix` and a successful retry
  - [ ] Test asserts createBeadsFromVBrief returns `success: true` and per-item create mocks were invoked
  - [ ] Returned errors array contains no message referencing 'stale local-DB artifacts'
  - [ ] Test passes under `vitest run`

### Update skills/beads/SKILL.md: document `bd ping`, `bd doctor --fix`, `bd prune`, `bd dolt push --remote` (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] skills/beads/SKILL.md mentions `bd ping --json` and `bd doctor --fix` with usage examples
  - [ ] skills/beads/SKILL.md mentions `bd prune` and `bd dolt push --remote`
  - [ ] No surviving references to v1.0.2 in the skill front matter or body
  - [ ] `npm run lint` passes

### Update skills/beads-panopticon-guide/SKILL.md with the worktree redirect story for v1.0.4 (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Doc explains both the redirect-based hydration path Panopticon uses and the auto-import path beads v1.0.4 supports
  - [ ] Doc reiterates the `never bd init in a redirect-managed worktree` rule
  - [ ] `npm run lint` passes

review.notes: Review role spawn failed: Workspace docker stack for PAN-1111 is not healthy: No Docker containers found for workspace stack pan-1111. Run 'pan workspace rebuild PAN-1111' or retry with --host to override.

history.notes: Review role spawn failed: Workspace docker stack for PAN-1111 is not healthy: No Docker containers found for workspace stack pan-1111. Run 'pan workspace rebuild PAN-1111' or retry with --host to override.

narratives.Problem: Panopticon ships beads v1.0.2 (released 2026-04-15). Latest is v1.0.4 (2026-05-09). v1.0.3 and v1.0.4 add native fixes for failure modes we currently work around in wrapper code: corrupt-manifest auto-recovery (the PAN-457 symptom), atomic audit-log writes, a `bd ping` connectivity probe, `bd doctor --fix` recovery, and 0700 perms repair. PR #1114 was closed without merge — the actual upgrade was never delivered: `bd --version` still reports 1.0.2, the install pin in src/cli/commands/install.ts is still 1.0.2, and createBeadsFromVBrief still uses a hand-rolled `bd list --json --limit 0` probe with a multi-branch stale-artifact recovery heuristic. The wrapper scaffolding now fights native beads features.
narratives.Proposal: Re-do the upgrade with discipline. (1) Bump the install version pin from 1.0.2 to 1.0.4 so `pan install` fetches the new binary and the version-guard warns below 1.0.4. (2) Replace the connectivity probe in createBeadsFromVBrief with `bd ping --json`. (3) Simplify the recovery surface: on probe failure, run `bd doctor --fix` once and retry the probe; if it still fails, return a clear error. Delete the stale-artifact heuristic — v1.0.3 auto-recovers from corrupt manifests, and v1.0.4 repairs permissions, so the heuristic is moot. (4) Update create-beads.test.ts to match the new recovery surface. (5) Add an integration test that simulates a corrupted local dolt manifest scenario in a worktree and verifies the wrapper relies on beads' own auto-recovery (acceptance criterion #5). (6) Update the two beads skill docs to document `bd ping`, `bd doctor --fix`, `bd prune`, and `bd dolt push --remote`. Explicitly out of scope per the issue body: removing `withBdMutex`, replacing `.beads/redirect` with auto-import, changing committed `.beads/metadata.json`/`hooks/` state. Those are deferred for a follow-up issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep withBdMutex in place; do not remove in this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body explicitly lists `withBdMutex` removal as out of scope ("keep it for now, revisit after the rest of this lands"). v1.0.3's atomic audit-log fix removes one class of races but does not eliminate the dolt-lock contention from concurrent `bd list` calls that withBdMutex serializes against (PAN-939). Removing it requires its own validation pass.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep workspace-manager.ts redirect setup; do not switch to v1.0.4 auto-import in this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Work item is labeled "Evaluate", not "Replace". Auto-import-after-pull/checkout is a behavioral change that affects every worktree's DB hydration; it needs a manual test pass before deleting the redirect-write path. Out-of-scope per issue ("Migrating committed .beads/issues.jsonl semantics ... verify behavior before changing committed state").

autoDecisions.summary: Keep committed .beads/metadata.json and .beads/hooks/; do not gitignore or remove in this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Work item is labeled "Decide". Changing committed state requires a separate verification cycle and a clear migration path. v1.0.4 writes them with 0700 perms, so the noise is gone regardless of commit status. Defer the commit-vs-ignore decision.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not add `bd batch` usage in done-preflight.ts.
autoDecisions.rationale: done-preflight.ts contains no bulk-close path — only `bd list --status open` diagnostics. There is no existing call site to convert. File a follow-up issue if a bulk-close path is introduced.

autoDecisions.summary: Acceptance test for v1.0.3 corruption-recovery (AC #5) lives in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts as a mocked-execFile test, not a live `bd` subprocess test.
autoDecisions.rationale: The repo's existing create-beads tests mock execFile/execFileAsync rather than shelling out to the real `bd`. Matching the established pattern keeps the test fast and deterministic on CI. The behavior under test is the wrapper's reaction (probe → doctor --fix → retry) to a corrupted-DB error, not beads' internal recovery — that is validated by beads' own test suite.

autoDecisions.summary: Work-role model is gpt-5.5 (per issue update).
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly states "Work agent will run on gpt-5.5." Plan beads are sized accordingly — small, mechanical changes with one bead carrying a foundation inspection gate so a less-context-rich harness has a checkpoint after the recovery rewrite.

autoDecisions.summary: Overwrote the existing 1-item auto-synthesized canonical spec on main to materialize the real 7-bead plan.
autoDecisions.rationale: The prior `pan start --auto` flow wrote a placeholder canonical spec at .pan/specs/2026-05-12-PAN-1111-...vbrief.json with a single auto-start: "Implement issue" item. `pan plan --auto` was invoked specifically to re-plan, and per PAN-1124 the pipeline only reads the main canonical spec. The canonicalFilename is preserved; only plan content is updated. This is consistent with the issue body's explicit re-plan request.

items.title: Bump bd install version pin from 1.0.2 to 1.0.4

narrative.Action: Edit src/cli/commands/install.ts (current pin at line 411: `recommendedVersion = 1 * 10000 + 0 * 100 + 2`). Change the trailing `+ 2` to `+ 4`, update the surrounding human-readable message from `v1.0.2+ recommended` to `v1.0.4+ recommended`, and grep the file for any other `1.0.2` literal that needs to be bumped (download URLs, comment references). After editing, run `pan install` locally so the developer machine moves to bd 1.0.4 before downstream beads execute.

`bd --version` on the developer machine reports 1.0.4 (or later patch on 1.0.x) after running pan install
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

items.title: Replace `bd list --json --limit 0` connectivity probe with `bd ping --json`

narrative.Action: In src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts createBeadsFromVBrief (current probe at line ~131: `execFileAsync('bd', ['list', '--json', '--limit', '0'], ...)`), swap the argv to `['ping', '--json']`. Same 8s timeout, same cwd. This bead is intent-only; recovery logic stays unchanged here (the next bead simplifies it). Update any test mocks that assert on the probe argv so the test suite still passes.

subItems.title: The connectivity probe in createBeadsFromVBrief uses `bd ping --json` (not `bd list`)
Probe timeout remains 8s; cwd remains workspacePath
Mocks in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts updated; `vitest run src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts` passes

items.title: Simplify createBeadsFromVBrief recovery: on probe failure, run `bd doctor --fix` once then retry

narrative.Action: Rewrite the catch block in createBeadsFromVBrief (currently src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts:134-192). New shape: on `bd ping` failure, log a one-line diagnostic, invoke `bd doctor --fix` (timeout ~30s, cwd workspacePath, errors swallowed but logged), then re-attempt `bd ping --json`. If the retry succeeds, fall through and proceed with bead creation. If the retry still fails, return `{ success: false, created: [], errors: [<one-line>], beadIds }`. DELETE: the `staleArtifacts` array, the `presentStale` filter, the dolt-residue diagnostic, and the redirect-exists vs. main-beads-exists branch logic. KEEP: the fresh-install fallback — if neither a `.beads/redirect` nor a project-root `.beads/` exists, run `bd init --prefix <derived>` followed by `git config beads.role contributor` and `bd config set export.git-add false`, with identical argv to today. Add a 3-5 line comment above the catch block explaining why the heuristic is gone (refs beads v1.0.3 dolt auto-recovery + v1.0.4 perms repair). The work.inspect gate runs here because every subsequent bead (tests, integration test) builds on this exact control flow — a wrong rewrite cascades.

subItems.title: On `bd ping` failure, the code invokes `bd doctor --fix` exactly once before retrying the probe
The stale-artifact heuristic (staleArtifacts array, presentStale check, dolt-residue diagnostic) is deleted from src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts
The redirect-vs-main-beads branch logic in the catch block is collapsed into a single retry/error-return path
The fresh-install bd-init fallback (no redirect, no main .beads/) is preserved with identical argv and follow-on `git config` / `bd config set` calls
A code comment near createBeadsFromVBrief explains why the heuristic was removed (refs beads v1.0.3 dolt auto-recovery)
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

items.title: Update create-beads.test.ts to match the simplified recovery surface

narrative.Action: Edit src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts. Remove tests that exercise the deleted stale-artifact heuristic (any case asserting on `metadata.json` / `embeddeddolt` / `presentStale` diagnostics). Add a positive test: probe fails on first call, `bd doctor --fix` is invoked, probe succeeds on retry, function proceeds and returns `success: true`. Add a negative test: probe fails, `bd doctor --fix` runs, probe still fails on retry → function returns `success: false` with a non-empty `errors` array. Verify the test file no longer references the old `['list', '--json', '--limit', '0']` argv anywhere.

subItems.title: All mocks reference `bd ping` (with `--json`), not the old `bd list --json --limit 0` probe
Tests for the stale-artifact heuristic are removed (no remaining references to `metadata.json` / `embeddeddolt` / `presentStale`)
There is a test asserting `bd doctor --fix` is called once between a failed probe and a successful retry, and the function returns `success: true`
There is a test asserting `success: false` with non-empty `errors` when both probe attempts fail
`vitest run src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts` passes

narrative.Action: Add a test case to src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts that models the PAN-457 recurrence scenario. Setup: a temp workspace containing a `.beads/redirect` file and a valid spec.vbrief.json; mock `bd ping --json` to throw with stderr `table not found: issues` on first call; mock `bd doctor --fix` to resolve; mock `bd ping --json` to resolve on second call; mock subsequent `bd create` calls. Assert: createBeadsFromVBrief returns `success: true`, the per-item `bd create` mock was invoked once per plan item, and the returned `errors` array contains nothing mentioning 'stale local-DB artifacts'. The test locks in the wrapper's reliance on beads-native auto-recovery rather than ever reintroducing the heuristic.

subItems.title: Test simulates a `table not found: issues` probe failure followed by `bd doctor --fix` and a successful retry
Test asserts createBeadsFromVBrief returns `success: true` and per-item create mocks were invoked
Returned errors array contains no message referencing 'stale local-DB artifacts'
Test passes under `vitest run`

items.title: Update skills/beads/SKILL.md: document `bd ping`, `bd doctor --fix`, `bd prune`, `bd dolt push --remote`

narrative.Action: Edit skills/beads/SKILL.md to (a) bump any version metadata header that references 1.0.2 to 1.0.4, (b) add a 'Health and recovery' section documenting `bd ping --json` as the canonical connectivity probe and `bd doctor --fix` as the canonical repair, (c) document `bd prune` for closed-bead hygiene, (d) document the `bd dolt push --remote <name>` flag. Keep the rest of the skill unchanged. Run `npm run lint` afterwards — the skills linter cross-checks documented flags against `bd --help` output.

items.title: Update skills/beads-panopticon-guide/SKILL.md with the worktree redirect story for v1.0.4

narrative.Action: Update skills/beads-panopticon-guide/SKILL.md to: (a) note that v1.0.4 adds a post-pull/checkout auto-import hook that hydrates the worktree DB from committed issues.jsonl; (b) explain that Panopticon still uses the manual `.beads/redirect` mechanism (rationale: deferred to a follow-up issue per PAN-1111 autoDecisions); (c) restate the rule — never `bd init` inside a redirect-managed worktree (referenced from commit 7d0e50cf3).
